section for tips on how to use the machine properly.

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
How should you store the paper to prevent it from curling or folding?
Do not store the paper in such a way that may cause it to curl or fold.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-009

You can load the paper into the paper drawer or multi-purpose tray. Load the paper you usually use into the paper
drawer. The paper drawer is convenient when using large amounts of paper. Use the multi-purpose tray when you
Available Paper(P. 194) for temporarily use size or type of paper that is not loaded in the paper drawer. See
available paper sizes.

![Loading Paper](images/image_p24_00.png)

Paper Type and Setting for the Machine
See the table below to specify the paper settings according to the type and weight of the paper to load into the
paper source. Specifying For more information about how to specify the paper settings on the machine, see
Paper Size and Type(P. 34) . For the amount of paper that can be loaded in each paper source, see
Available Paper(P. 194) .

![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p24_01.png)


![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p24_02.png)


![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p25_00.png)

● Highly textured paper

● Glossy paper

**Paper handling and storage**

● Store the paper on a flat surface.

● Keep the paper wrapped in its original package to protect the paper from moisture or dryness.

● Do not store the paper in such a way that may cause it to curl or fold.

● Do not store the paper vertically or stack too much paper.

● Do not store the paper in direct sunlight, or in a place subject to high humidity, dryness, or drastic
changes in temperature or humidity.

![● Do not store the paper in](images/image_p25_01.png)

**Registering a Custom Paper Size(P. 36)**

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
Why should users set the necessary configurations for their printer?

Users must configure the machine to counter risks of malicious third parties gaining unauthorized access or exploiting negligence, which could lead to leaks of confidential information and unanticipated losses.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01Y

Confidential information is handled by information devices everywhere, including computers and printers, and any of
these devices may become a target for malicious third parties at anytime. Attackers may directly gain unauthorized
access to your devices, or indirectly take advantage of negligence or improper use. Either way, you may incur
unanticipated losses when your confidential information is leaked. To counter these risks, the machine is equipped
with a variety of security functions. Set the necessary configuration depending on your environment.

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
How can you prevent moisture evaporation from damp paper in the machine?
You can prevent this by using a stack of new unopened paper.

provenance.chunk: Steam is Emitted from the Machine's Output Area. (chunk 0)

chunk_text:
● Use only dry paper in the machine. Moisture evaporates from damp paper due to the heat generated
inside the fixing assembly (it is most likely to occur at low room temperatures). This is not a malfunction,
but you can prevent this by using a stack of new unopened paper. However, if you smell something
burning, immediately turn OFF the machine, unplug the power plug from the AC power outlet, and contact
your local authorized Canon dealer. Continued use may lead to a fire or electrical shock.

turning OFF the machine.

To copy multiple pages, refer to 'Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on 1)(P. 53)'. To print from a computer, refer to 'Printing from a Computer(P. 66)', which supports up to 4 pages for copies and up to 16 pages for print jobs.

**Print Multiple Pages on a Single Sheet**

To really start saving paper, shrink multiple pages onto a single sheet - 2 or 4 pages for copies, and up to 16
pages for print jobs sent from your computer.

![To really start saving paper, shrink multiple](images/image_p188_02.png)

To copy multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on
1)(P. 53) 1/4 on

**Sleep Mode**

For saving energy and money, a sleep mode that powers down a machine when nobody is using it has become
an essential feature. This machine will enter sleep mode automatically, and can also be put to sleep whenever
once to put the machine to sleep. Press it again to instantly wake the machine back into you want. Press
action.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How can I perform a keyword search in this manual?

To perform a search, enter a keyword (such as "envelope" or "toner cartridge") or phrases (like "connecting to a network"); the pages containing those terms will be displayed so you can find what you are looking for. You can also use AND Boolean logic to search for multiple keywords.

chunk_text:
● Perform a search by entering a keyword, such as "envelope" or "toner cartridge," and the pages containing
the keyword are displayed. You can find the page you are looking for from those results. You can also enter
phrases such as "connecting to a network." The keyword search also supports the AND Boolean to search for
multiple keywords. For the organization of the search screen and how to operate the search screen, see
Search(P. 205) .

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
How should this product be disposed of?

This product should not be disposed of with household waste; instead, it must be handed over to a designated collection point, such as an authorized one-for-one basis when buying a new similar product or to an authorized collection site for recycling waste electrical and electronic equipment (EEE) and batteries.

chunk_text:
![◼ Directives WEEE and Battery](images/image_p216_01.png)

Only for European Union and EEA (Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein)

These symbols indicate that this product is not to be disposed of with your household waste, according to the WEEE
Directive (2012/19/EU), the Battery Directive (2006/66/EC) and/or national legislation implementing those Directives.
If a chemical symbol is printed beneath the symbol shown above, in accordance with the Battery Directive, this
indicates that a heavy metal (Hg = Mercury, Cd = Cadmium, Pb = Lead) is present in this battery or accumulator at a
concentration above an applicable threshold specified in the Battery Directive.
This product should be handed over to a designated collection point, e.g., on an authorized one-for-one basis when
you buy a new similar product or to an authorized collection site for recycling waste electrical and electronic
equipment (EEE) and batteries and accumulators. Improper handling of this type of waste could have a possible
impact on the environment and human health due to potentially hazardous substances that are generally associated
with EEE. Your cooperation in the correct disposal of this product will contribute to the effective usage of natural
resources.
For more information about the recycling of this product, please contact your local city office, waste authority,
approved scheme or your household waste disposal service or visit www.canon-europe.com/weee, or www.canon-
europe.com/battery.

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
What is the proper method for storing paper to avoid curling or folding?
Do not store the paper in such a way that may cause it to curl or fold.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-009

You can load the paper into the paper drawer or multi-purpose tray. Load the paper you usually use into the paper
drawer. The paper drawer is convenient when using large amounts of paper. Use the multi-purpose tray when you
Available Paper(P. 194) for temporarily use size or type of paper that is not loaded in the paper drawer. See
available paper sizes.

![Loading Paper](images/image_p24_00.png)

Paper Type and Setting for the Machine
See the table below to specify the paper settings according to the type and weight of the paper to load into the
paper source. Specifying For more information about how to specify the paper settings on the machine, see
Paper Size and Type(P. 34) . For the amount of paper that can be loaded in each paper source, see
Available Paper(P. 194) .

![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p24_01.png)


![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p24_02.png)


![Paper Type and Setting for the Machine](images/image_p25_00.png)

● Highly textured paper

● Glossy paper

**Paper handling and storage**

● Store the paper on a flat surface.

● Keep the paper wrapped in its original package to protect the paper from moisture or dryness.

● Do not store the paper in such a way that may cause it to curl or fold.

● Do not store the paper vertically or stack too much paper.

● Do not store the paper in direct sunlight, or in a place subject to high humidity, dryness, or drastic
changes in temperature or humidity.

![● Do not store the paper in](images/image_p25_01.png)

**Registering a Custom Paper Size(P. 36)**

side face up).

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
What actions should be taken when loading paper that differs from the current load?
You must specify the paper size and type settings to match the paper that is loaded.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-00H

You must specify the paper size and type settings to match the paper that is loaded. Make sure to change the paper
settings when you load the paper that is different from the previously loaded paper. You can also register a default
paper size and paper type for the multi-purpose tray, which is convenient if you always load paper of the same size
and type.

![Specifying Paper Size and Type](images/image_p38_00.png)

What is the procedure for configuring the device to power off automatically following a defined duration of inactivity?

chunk_text:
3EEJ-00L

By enabling this setting, you can set the machine to automatically shut down. The machine automatically turns OFF
after the specified period of idle time has elapsed since entering the sleep mode. You can cut electric power
consumption at night and on holidays if you forget to turn OFF the machine.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
What is the procedure to save commonly used copy configurations on the printer?

To register settings, select <Copy>, then <Favorite Settings>, and finally <Regist./Edit>. Next, choose a setting slot from <Settings 1> to <Settings 4>, configure each desired value (such as number of copies or sharpness), press <Apply>, and confirm with <Yes>.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01A

You can register up to four frequently used combinations of copy settings. By simply selecting a registered setting
combination when it is needed, you can eliminate the need to configure the same settings every time you make
copies, such as if you frequently set the number of copies to 5, set <Erase Frame> to <On>, and increase the sharpness
when copying. You can register the setting combinations as <Settings 1> to <Settings 4>.

![Registering Frequently Used Copy Settings](images/image_p67_00.png)

<Copy> <Favorite Settings> <Regist./Edit> Select <Settings 1> to <Settings 4> Select each
setting and set the value <Apply> <Yes>

![<Copy> <Favorite Settings> <Regist./Edit> Select <Settings 1>](images/image_p67_01.png)

time you print is time consuming. If you register your frequently used combinations of print settings as "profiles," you
can specify print settings simply by selecting one of the profiles from the list. This section explains how to register
profiles and how to print using profiles.

3EEJ-01L

The default settings are the settings that are first displayed whenever you open the print settings screen of the printer
driver. You can change these settings to the settings that you use most often. For example, if you always want to print
2 document pages on each printout page, set [2 on 1] as the default page layout setting. From the next time that you
open the printer driver screen, the setting will always be [2 on 1], eliminating the need to change this setting every
time you make prints.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
What methods exist for scanning original documents?
Original documents can be scanned by using the control panel of the machine or an application on a computer.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01R

You can scan original documents by using the control panel of the machine or an application on a computer.
Whichever way you use, the scanned documents are converted into electronic file formats such as PDFs. Use the scan
function to convert large-volume paper documents into files so that you can organize them easily.

Using MF Scan Utility and using an application such as an image-processing or word-processing application.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01U

processing or word-processing application.

![Scanning from a Computer](images/image_p87_00.png)

**Scanning Using MF Scan Utility**

MF Scan Utility is an application included with the machine that enables you to scan
documents or images to a computer. You can forward the scanned data to a specified
application, or attach it to an e-mail message. For more information, click [Instructions]
on MF Scan Utility.

3EEJ-01W

You can scan originals from applications such as image-processing or word-processing
software. The scanned image is directly loaded in the application, allowing you to edit
or process the image immediately. The following procedure varies depending on the

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
What is the reason for users to configure their printer settings?

Users must configure the machine to counter risks of malicious third parties gaining unauthorized access or exploiting negligence, which could lead to leaks of confidential information and unanticipated losses.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01Y

Confidential information is handled by information devices everywhere, including computers and printers, and any of
these devices may become a target for malicious third parties at anytime. Attackers may directly gain unauthorized
access to your devices, or indirectly take advantage of negligence or improper use. Either way, you may incur
unanticipated losses when your confidential information is leaked. To counter these risks, the machine is equipped
with a variety of security functions. Set the necessary configuration depending on your environment.

chunk_text:
If a key is not pressed for a specified period of time, the screen and the settings are initialized to display the default
screen (Auto Reset). Specify the interval at which the Auto Reset is performed. Select <0> to disable the Auto Reset
function. The Auto Reset is not performed in the following cases:

● The setting menu is displayed.

● The machine is processing data, such as when printing documents.

● An error message is displayed and the [Error] indicator is blinking (However, Auto Reset is performed when the
error does not prevent the user from continuing to use a function.)

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
In what way can one reach the Special Printing Mode M configuration?
Navigate to Menu > Adjustment/Maintenance > Special Processing > Special Printing Mode M.

chunk_text:
Change the <Special Printing Mode M> setting if there is a degradation in print quality or irregularity in print
density. This mode enables you to select one of five levels for the machine's transfer output. Select a higher
mode number if you want to print on paper to which toner does not adhere well, such as index cards. Select a
lower mode number when printing on paper that has been stored at high temperature and high humidity for a
long time.


![Special Printing Mode M](images/image_p115_00.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode M>

Select the mode

Special Printing Mode N
Change the <Special Printing Mode N> setting if paper curls or jams when printing on paper that has absorbed
moisture. If <Mode 3> is set, the effect of this setting is maximized, but it may take the machine longer to print.

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_01.png)

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_02.png)

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_03.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode N>

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What steps should I take to minimize print wait times after changing to a larger paper size?
Set the Special Printing Mode S setting to Mode 1 or Mode 2; if Mode 1 is set, the waiting time is minimized.

chunk_text:
If you switch to a larger size of paper than previously used, it may take a long time for the machine to resume
reduce the waiting printing. In this case, set the <Special Printing Mode S> setting to <Mode 1> or <Mode 2> to
time for the next print. If <Mode 1> is set, waiting time is minimized.


![Special Printing Mode S](images/image_p116_00.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode S>

Set the <Special Printing Mode P> setting to <On> if paper curls when printing on a lightweight paper.**

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
What steps should be taken when the control panel's paper size setting differs from the actual paper loaded?

Match the paper size setting specified in <Paper Settings> and the size of the paper that is actually loaded by changing the <Paper Settings> to match the loaded paper size, or load paper with the size set to <Paper Settings>. If printing from a computer, check whether the paper size setting in the printer driver matches the loaded paper size.

chunk_text:

Change the setting of <Paper Settings> to match the loaded paper size.

If printing from a computer, check whether the paper size setting in the printer driver matches the loaded
paper size.

Load the paper with the size that is set to <Paper Settings>.

chunk_text:

If printing is not successful or another error occurs, that error appears on a message on the <Status Monitor> screen
as a three-digit number (error code). Check the following to find out causes and solutions for each error code. For
<Status Monitor> Screen(P. 11) . details of the <Status Monitor> screen, see

**Memory is full.**

**An operation that caused a job to be canceled has been performed.**

● When you cancel a job, this error code is displayed, but this is not abnormal. Print documents again as
necessary.

**The machine turns OFF for some reason.**

power plug is inserted into an outlet firmly. ● Check whether the

**A document could not be printed because its data size exceeded the machine's upper processing
capability.**

● The machine may not be able to print documents with many pages. Reduce the number of pages per
printing, or print when no print job is queued or no document is being printed. If you are still unable to
print, check the document data for any defects.

Printing was canceled from the computer.
● If you cancel an operation when the print data is being sent to the machine from a computer, this error
code is displayed, but this is not abnormal. Print again as necessary.

**You tried to print a file that is either corrupted or not supported.
● Check the file and try again.

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What steps should be taken to stop moisture from evaporating due to damp paper inside the device?
You can prevent this by using a stack of new unopened paper.

provenance.chunk: Steam is Emitted from the Machine's Output Area. (chunk 0)

chunk_text:
● Use only dry paper in the machine. Moisture evaporates from damp paper due to the heat generated
inside the fixing assembly (it is most likely to occur at low room temperatures). This is not a malfunction,
but you can prevent this by using a stack of new unopened paper. However, if you smell something
burning, immediately turn OFF the machine, unplug the power plug from the AC power outlet, and contact
your local authorized Canon dealer. Continued use may lead to a fire or electrical shock.

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
How can one fix problems related to curled paper?

Check usable paper and replace it with appropriate paper, specify the paper size and type settings properly, or change the <Paper Type> setting to one for thinner paper (e.g., changing from <Plain 2> to <Plain 1>).

chunk_text:
![Paper Curls](images/image_p149_01.png)

● Check usable paper, and replace with appropriate paper. Also, specify the paper size and type settings
properly.
Available Paper(P. 194)
Loading Paper(P. 20)
Specifying Paper Size and Type(P. 34)
● Change the setting of <Paper Type> to a setting for thinner paper. For example, if the current setting is
<Plain 2 (76-90 g/m²)>, change it to <Plain 1 (64-75 g/m²)>.


**Specifying Paper Size and Type(P. 34)
Available Paper(P. 194)**

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What is the method to fit several pages onto one page when copying or printing?

To copy multiple pages, refer to 'Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on 1)(P. 53)'. To print from a computer, refer to 'Printing from a Computer(P. 66)', which supports up to 4 pages for copies and up to 16 pages for print jobs.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-041

![Going Green and Saving Money](images/image_p188_00.png)

![3EEJ-041](images/image_p188_01.png)

**Print Multiple Pages on a Single Sheet**

To really start saving paper, shrink multiple pages onto a single sheet - 2 or 4 pages for copies, and up to 16
pages for print jobs sent from your computer.

![To really start saving paper, shrink multiple](images/image_p188_02.png)

To copy multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on
1)(P. 53) 1/4 on
To print multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Printing from a Computer(P. 66)

![To copy multiple pages of a document](images/image_p188_03.png)

**Sleep Mode**

For saving energy and money, a sleep mode that powers down a machine when nobody is using it has become
an essential feature. This machine will enter sleep mode automatically, and can also be put to sleep whenever
once to put the machine to sleep. Press it again to instantly wake the machine back into you want. Press
action.

![For saving energy and money, a sleep](images/image_p188_04.png)

**For more information about this function, see Entering Sleep Mode(P. 39) .**

![For more information about this function, see](images/image_p188_05.png)

**Save Toner**

Got multi-page documents or drafts that don't need presentation-quality printing?


There's a setting that uses less toner. Give it a try and save toner and money.

![There's a setting that uses less toner.](images/image_p189_00.png)

**For more information about this function, see Printing from a Computer(P. 66) .**

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
What is the easiest way to define the page layout and binding location?
You can intuitively specify these settings on the preview image in the printer driver using simple click-operations while viewing how they are applied.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-042

![Improving Efficiency](images/image_p190_00.png)

![3EEJ-042](images/image_p190_01.png)

**Make Print Settings Quickly with Intuitive Operation**

You can easily specify the page layout as well as binding position, etc. intuitively on the preview image in the

*2 Copy/print speeds are measured based on internal testing using A4 size paper copied/printed with 100% copy/print

content on plain paper (copy speed is tested by copying on the platen glass). Copy/print speeds may vary depending on
paper type, paper size or paper feeding direction.

a certain unit or limiting impact to image quality during continuous copying/printing.

*3 May vary depending on the installation environment and paper to use.

What is the method for conducting a keyword search within this manual?

To perform a search, enter a keyword (such as "envelope" or "toner cartridge") or phrases (like "connecting to a network"); the pages containing those terms will be displayed so you can find what you are looking for. You can also use AND Boolean logic to search for multiple keywords.

the keyword are displayed. You can find the page you are looking for from those results. You can also enter
phrases such as "connecting to a network." The keyword search also supports the AND Boolean to search for
multiple keywords. For the organization of the search screen and how to operate the search screen, see
Search(P. 205) .

To perform a search, enter a keyword (such as "envelope" or "toner cartridge") or phrases (like "connecting to a network"); the pages containing those terms will be displayed so you can find what you are looking for. You can also use AND Boolean logic to search for multiple keywords.

chunk_text:
● Perform a search by entering a keyword, such as "envelope" or "toner cartridge," and the pages containing
the keyword are displayed. You can find the page you are looking for from those results. You can also enter
phrases such as "connecting to a network." The keyword search also supports the AND Boolean to search for
multiple keywords. For the organization of the search screen and how to operate the search screen, see
Search(P. 205) .

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What are the proper disposal methods for this product?

This product should not be disposed of with household waste; instead, it must be handed over to a designated collection point, such as an authorized one-for-one basis when buying a new similar product or to an authorized collection site for recycling waste electrical and electronic equipment (EEE) and batteries.

chunk_text:
![◼ Directives WEEE and Battery](images/image_p216_01.png)

Only for European Union and EEA (Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein)

These symbols indicate that this product is not to be disposed of with your household waste, according to the WEEE
Directive (2012/19/EU), the Battery Directive (2006/66/EC) and/or national legislation implementing those Directives.
If a chemical symbol is printed beneath the symbol shown above, in accordance with the Battery Directive, this
indicates that a heavy metal (Hg = Mercury, Cd = Cadmium, Pb = Lead) is present in this battery or accumulator at a
concentration above an applicable threshold specified in the Battery Directive.
This product should be handed over to a designated collection point, e.g., on an authorized one-for-one basis when
you buy a new similar product or to an authorized collection site for recycling waste electrical and electronic
equipment (EEE) and batteries and accumulators. Improper handling of this type of waste could have a possible
impact on the environment and human health due to potentially hazardous substances that are generally associated
with EEE. Your cooperation in the correct disposal of this product will contribute to the effective usage of natural
resources.
For more information about the recycling of this product, please contact your local city office, waste authority,
approved scheme or your household waste disposal service or visit www.canon-europe.com/weee, or www.canon-
europe.com/battery.

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
According to Canon, what is their recommendation concerning the extension of Activation Times beyond default configurations?
Canon does not recommend extending the Activation Times from the optimum ones set as default.

chunk_text: Reducing your environmental impact while saving money. Power Consumption and Activation Time. The amount of electricity a device consumes depends on the way the device is used. This product is designed and set. in a way to allow you to reduce your electricity costs. After the last print it switches to Ready Mode. In this mode it can. print again immediately if required. If the product is not used for a time, the device switches to its Power Save Mode.. The devices consume less power (Watt) in these modes.. If you wish to set a longer Activation Time or would like to completely deactivate the Power Save Mode, please. consider that this device may then only switch to a lower energy level after a longer period of time or not at all.. Canon does not recommend extending the Activation Times from the optimum ones set as default.. Energy Star ®. ® The Energy Star programme is a voluntary scheme to promote the development and purchase of energy efficient. models, which help to minimise environmental impact.. ® Products which meet the stringent requirements of the Energy Star programme for both environmental benefits and. ® the amount of energy consumption will carry the Energy Star logo accordingly.. Paper types. This product can be used to print on both recycled and virgin paper (certified to an environmental stewardship. scheme), which complies with EN12281 or a similar quality standard. In addition it can support printing on media. down to a weight of 64g/m².. Lighter paper means less resources used and a lower environmental footprint for your printing needs.. Duplex printing (Standard duplex printing products only).

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
According to Canon, what actions are strongly advised against concerning the duplex printing feature?
Canon strongly recommends that you do not disable the duplex printing function.

chunk_text: Duplex printing (Standard duplex printing products only). Duplex printing capability enables you to print on both sides automatically, and therefore helps to reduce the use of. valuable resources by reducing your paper consumption.. The duplex function has been auto enabled during the set up and driver installation and Canon strongly recommends. that you do not disable this function. You should continue to use the duplex function to reduce the environmental. impact of your work with this product at all times.
